sections.heading: 1. Calling Java from Kotlin
sections.content: Kotlin can call Java code seamlessly. Kotlin adds syntactic sugar to make Java APIs feel more natural.

sections.heading: Getters and Setters
sections.content: Java getter/setter methods are exposed as Kotlin properties.

sections.heading: Java Getters/Setters as Properties
sections.content: Kotlin treats Java getName()/setName() as a property.

sections.heading: Nullability & Platform Types
sections.content: Java does not enforce null safety. Kotlin treats Java types as platform types (e.g., String!). Always check @Nullable and @NonNull annotations and treat uncertain types as nullable.

sections.heading: 2. Calling Kotlin from Java
sections.content: Kotlin provides annotations to improve Java interoperability.

sections.heading: @JvmStatic
sections.content: Makes companion object functions appear as true static methods in Java.

sections.heading: @JvmStatic Example
sections.content: Exposes companion object method as static in Java.

sections.heading: @JvmOverloads
sections.content: Generates overloaded methods for functions with default parameters so Java can call them naturally.

sections.heading: @JvmOverloads Example
sections.content: Generates multiple overloaded methods for Java.

sections.heading: 3. Key Syntax Differences
sections.content: Major conceptual shifts when converting Java to Kotlin.

sections.heading: Java vs Kotlin
items: Semicolons: Required in Java, optional in Kotlin.
Variables: String name = "Alex"; vs val name: String = "Alex"
Ternary: a ? b : c vs if (a) b else c
Switch vs When: switch(x) vs when(x)
Casting: (String) obj vs obj as String
Static: static keyword vs companion object

sections.heading: 4. Any vs Object
sections.content: In Kotlin, the root type is Any instead of Object. Any does not include methods like wait() or notify(). Cast to java.lang.Object if needed.

sections.heading: 5. SAM Conversions
sections.content: If a Java interface has a single abstract method (SAM), Kotlin allows you to pass a lambda instead of an anonymous inner class.

sections.heading: SAM Conversion Example
sections.content: Replacing anonymous inner class with a lambda.

sections.heading: Summary
items: Kotlin and Java can coexist in the same project.
Use @JvmStatic and @JvmOverloads for Java-friendly Kotlin APIs.
Be careful with platform types and null safety.
Use SAM conversions to simplify Java interface usage.
